response.justification: Sarah brings 10+ years of polyglot full-stack engineering with direct hits on the primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS — across multiple senior roles. The current position at re:cinq is an especially strong signal: it explicitly names AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework work, which is a differentiator few candidates will have. The main gap is Nest.js, which is absent from the resume, and Python is not listed despite being a stated requirement. These are learnable gaps for a candidate of this seniority and breadth, making her a strong yes for an interview.
strengths: Direct TypeScript/Node.js/React/MongoDB/AWS experience across Optilyz and re:cinq, matching the primary stack closely
Active AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at current role — rare and directly relevant to the job's AI agent development requirement
Exceptionally deep testing culture: authored TDD libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), ran public TDD workshops, and applied TDD/CI/CD in production environments
concerns: Nest.js is entirely absent from the resume despite being a listed primary framework
Python is not mentioned anywhere — required proficiency per the job description
Modern JS testing frameworks (Vitest, Playwright, Jest) are not named; testing experience is largely in the DLang ecosystem
